summary: Completed a matching C decompilation of Fox/Falco up special, moving the Firefox/Firebird SpecialHi action-state callbacks and helpers out of raw assembly into src/melee/ft/chara/ftFox/ftFox_SpecialHi.c. The PR added action IDs, attribute names, collision/action-state flag macros, prototypes, and small fighter/header helpers needed by the implementation. It also renamed Falco action table entries to the newly matched ftFox_SpecialHi* symbols, added small Fox Reflector GFX helper stubs in ftFox_SpecialLw.c, and included minor Game & Watch Chef struct/field cleanup. There were no PR body comments or review comments in the slice, so intent beyond matching and symbol/header integration is inferred from the diff.
what_changed: Added a new 784-line src/melee/ft/chara/ftFox/ftFox_SpecialHi.c implementing Fox/Falco Firefox/Firebird up-special behavior in C.
Decompiled SpecialHi GFX helpers for charge, launch, and rebound effects, including ef_Spawn IDs 0x48B, 0x48C, and 0x406 plus hitlag pause/resume callbacks through efLib_PauseAll and efLib_ResumeAll.
Implemented grounded and aerial SpecialHi start actions, hold callbacks, launch callbacks, landing/fall callbacks, rebound callbacks, and ground/air transition handlers.

Added named action-state enum entries in ftfox.h: AS_FOX_SPECIALHI_HOLD, AS_FOX_SPECIALHI_HOLDAIR, AS_FOX_SPECIALHI, AS_FOX_SPECIALAIRHI, AS_FOX_SPECIALHI_LANDING, AS_FOX_SPECIALHI_FALL, and AS_FOX_SPECIALHI_BOUND.
Added FTFOX_SPECIALHI_COLL_FLAG as a composite Fighter_ActionStateChange flag set used during Firefox/Firebird ground-air transitions.
Renamed and typed Fox SpecialHi attribute fields in ftFoxAttributes, including gravity delay, speed, duration, bounce variable, freefall mobility, landing lag, and bound angle.

Updated Falco's assembly action table to reference the shared ftFox_SpecialHi* callback names instead of anonymous func_800E7354-style labels, reflecting that Falco's Firebird uses these Fox SpecialHi routines.
Reduced raw assembly coverage in asm/melee/ft/chara/ftfox.s while preserving remaining unmatched assembly in the file.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftFox/ftFox_SpecialLw.c with three reflector GFX helper functions for effect IDs 0x488, 0x489, and 0x48A.
Added fighter.h convenience helpers and constants: IS_INTERRUPTIBLE, CLIFFCATCH_BOTH/RIGHT/LEFT, getFtColl, and ftGetGroundAir.
Renamed Game & Watch Chef state fields from generic x2240/x2244 to x2240_chefVar1/x2244_chefVar2, added ftGameWatchChef, and adjusted Chef max attribute type from s32 to f32.
Updated obj_files.mk to include the newly decompiled C object(s) and reflect the asm-to-C build split.
smart_moves: Preserved Fox/Falco sharing explicitly: the Falco action table now points at ftFox_SpecialHiHold_Anim, ftFox_SpecialAirHi_Coll, and related shared callbacks instead of keeping address-style names.
Grouped the SpecialHi implementation by action-state lifecycle callbacks: Anim, IASA, Phys, Coll, plus StartAction and transition helpers, matching the standard fighter decomp organization.

Used semantic attribute names for the Firefox/Firebird data block, turning offsets like x54, x68, x90, and x94 into searchable fields such as x54_FOX_FIREFOX_GRAVITY_DELAY, x68_FOX_FIREFOX_DURATION, x90_FOX_FIREFOX_LANDING_LAG, and x94_FOX_FIREFOX_BOUND_ANGLE.
Introduced small inline/accessor helpers such as getFtColl and ftGetGroundAir to avoid repeating struct-offset expressions while still producing matching code.

Captured difficult matching shapes directly in C where needed, including goto-labelled collision-angle logic, redundant-looking assignments, explicit casts, and unusual comparisons that the author noted as nonsensical but matching.
Kept decomp.me scratch links and original addresses in comments for each recovered function, making future audit and regression work easier.
decomp_lessons: For shared character code, update both the implementation and every action table that references the shared callbacks; Falco's Firebird table needed the ftFox_SpecialHi* symbol names after Fox's C implementation was added.

Fighter special move decomp benefits from naming the full action-state sequence before polishing internals: Hold, HoldAir, Launch, AirLaunch, Landing, Fall, and Bound each map cleanly to Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll callbacks.

State variables near Fighter offsets x2340, x2344, x2348, x234C, and x2350 can become understandable once tied to move phases: gravity delay, angle, duration counter, end-duration counter, and bounce/contact counter in this PR.

Effect-spawn helpers often need to set x2219_flag.bits.b0 and hitlag enter/exit callbacks, then clear x21BC_callback_Accessory4; this pattern appears in both Firefox/Firebird and Reflector GFX helpers.

Matching collision-heavy functions may require preserving control-flow artifacts such as gotos, one-iteration do/while blocks, and seemingly redundant checks rather than rewriting to clean structured C.
When a C match uncovers generic constants, adding shared fighter.h macros such as CLIFFCATCH_BOTH and IS_INTERRUPTIBLE can make later fighter decomp code more readable and reusable.
Attribute field type corrections matter for matching: Game & Watch Chef max changed from s32 to f32 in the header, and Fox Firefox fields gained more precise names/types.
naming_conventions: Fox up-special functions use ftFox_SpecialHi* for grounded Firefox and ftFox_SpecialAirHi* for aerial variants.
Action callbacks follow the project convention of suffixing lifecycle role: _Anim, _IASA, _Phys, and _Coll.
Ground/air transition helpers are named with directional suffixes such as _GroundToAir and _AirToGround.
Action-state enum entries use AS_FOX_SPECIALHI_* and AS_FOX_SPECIALAIRHI for character-specific fighter states.
Fox special attributes are named with offset prefix plus move-specific semantic label, for example x64_FOX_FIREFOX_DIRECTION_STICK_RANGE_MIN and x84_FOX_FIREFOX_BOUND_VEL_X.
Composite action-state flags are named by character and move, as in FTFOX_SPECIALHI_COLL_FLAG.
Game & Watch special-state fields were made more semantic with x2240_chefVar1 and x2244_chefVar2 rather than bare offset names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced named assembly function references in action tables with newly matched C symbols while leaving unmatched neighboring functions as func_800E* labels.
Used explicit f32 constants for HALF_PI32 and DOUBLE_PI32 to match trigonometric angle code.
Used casts such as (s32), (u32)(s32), and explicit f32 casts around state variables and math expressions to control generated code.
Preserved odd source shapes where needed for matching, including comments noting that parts of ftFox_SpecialAirHi_Coll did not make logical sense but matched.
Used decomp.me scratch references per function as provenance for matching attempts.
Matched inline-style helpers for collision and ground/air access in fighter.h rather than only using raw struct field references.
Kept callback assignment order visible for effect helpers: spawn once via x2219_flag, set enter/exit hitlag callbacks, then clear or install accessory callbacks.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename lbl_800E83E0/lbl_800E845C/lbl_800E84D8 reflector GFX helpers to semantic ftFox_SpecialLw names.
Find later PRs that clean up or refactor ftFox_SpecialAirHi_Coll after this matching-first implementation.
Search for uses of FTFOX_SPECIALHI_COLL_FLAG to see whether the composite flag was reused or renamed.
Search for later changes to ftFoxAttributes x6C/x70/x8C/x94 Firefox field names to verify final naming.
Find PRs that remove remaining asm/melee/ft/chara/ftfox.s functions after SpecialHi was matched.
